varieties.name: Malayan Dwarf (All Colors)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree (30-60 feet), compact growth, early bearing in 3-4 years. Available in Yellow, Green, Golden/Orange, and Red varieties based on fruit color.
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Humid subtropical subtropical climate with protection. Requires warm, sheltered position, free-draining soil, and deep organic mulch. Resistant to Lethal Yellowing disease. Best planted in coastal locations; will tolerate temperatures to -3°C but unlikely to fruit south of northern NSW.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (Queensland), Oxley Nursery humid subtropical climates, specialized palm nurseries

varieties.name: Dwarf Hybrid (Solomon Islands selection)
varieties.origin: Solomon Islands, with some selections from North Queensland
varieties.traits: High-yielding precocious hybrid, flowers in 3rd year, fruits in 4th year in moist tropical conditions. Moderate-sized fruit with large volume-to-husk ratio.
varieties.growing_notes: Will grow outside the tropics in warm, sheltered position. Worth trying in coastal locations as far south as Coffs Harbour, NSW. Requires free-draining soil and deep organic mulch. Suitable for Humid subtropical warmer suburbs with frost protection.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (Queensland)

varieties.name: Maypan Hybrid
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree to 60 feet, cold-hardy for a coconut, disease-resistant especially to Lethal Yellowing. Combines hardiness of Malayan Dwarf with weather resistance of Panama Tall.

varieties.name: Fiji Dwarf
varieties.origin: Fiji, selected for disease resistance in 1970s
varieties.traits: Unique dwarf with swollen bole at base, slower-growing, distinctive thick crooked trunk, compact height to 25 feet. Shorter, wider leaflets create fuller canopy.
varieties.growing_notes: Selected for resistance to Lethal Yellowing disease. Suitable for humid subtropical climates with protection, especially in warmer coastal suburbs. Compact size makes harvesting easier. Requires warm microclimate and frost protection.
varieties.availability: Specialty palm nurseries

varieties.name: Native Niu Kafa Type (Historical)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Australia, documented in historical records from Four Mile Beach Port Douglas (1908), Russell Island, and other Queensland coastal areas
varieties.traits: Tall upright crown shape (distinct from spreading niu vai varieties). Long, angular three-sided nuts with thick husk adapted for oceanic dispersal. Some dwarf phenotypes exist with unique Australian characteristics.
varieties.yield_notes: Variable, generally lower than cultivated varieties

varieties.name: Panama Tall (Pacific Tall)
varieties.traits: Large tree to 100 feet, exceptional hardiness and cold tolerance for coconut. Most cold-resistant variety available. Excellent wind resistance.

varieties.name: West Coast Tall (WCT)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to India, cultivated since ancient times along west coast

varieties.name: Kuttiyadi (WCT Ecotype)
varieties.origin: Kuttiyadi region, Kozhikode District, tropical monsoon climates - ecotype of West Coast Tall
varieties.traits: Tall palm adapted to hilly midland regions, distinct genetic makeup from other WCT due to local environmental factors (climate, pH, rainfall). Matures in 5 years (faster than standard WCT).
varieties.yield_notes: Higher nut yield than standard WCT
varieties.growing_notes: Indigenous tropical monsoon climates ecotype adapted to midland regions with different environmental conditions than coastal WCT. Developed over generations through natural selection in Kuttiyadi's specific terroir. Represents local agrobiodiversity. Excellent for tropical monsoon climates area which is inland.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, regional nurseries in Kozhikode/Calicut area

varieties.name: East Coast Tall (ECT)
varieties.origin: East coast of India (Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha)
varieties.traits: Tall variety 50-90 feet, thrives in well-drained sandy loam, alluvial and red loamy soils. Distinct orange color on young nuts. Tolerates inland conditions better than WCT.
varieties.yield_notes: 60-70 nuts per year, starts bearing 6-8 years
varieties.growing_notes: Well-balanced water and meat content, excellent for both tender coconut water and mature processing. Moderately tolerant to pests (bugs, mites, insects). More tolerant of inland conditions than WCT, making it suitable for areas slightly away from coast. Good choice for Tropical monsoon inland location.
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates from Coconut Development Board, government nurseries

varieties.name: Lakshadweep Ordinary (LCT / Chandrakalpa)
varieties.origin: Lakshadweep Islands, introduced to Indian mainland in 1940
varieties.traits: Moderate height 11.6 meters, stem girth 80cm. Grows in all soil types, excellent moisture stress tolerance. Long productive life 50-80 years.
varieties.yield_notes: 100 nuts per palm annually, starts flowering 6 years after planting
varieties.growing_notes: Extremely versatile - grows well in all soil types from sandy beaches to inland areas. Superior toddy production makes it valuable for traditional tropical monsoon climates toddy/jaggery industry. High humidity and salt spray tolerance. Perfect for Tropical monsoon monsoon climate. Recommended throughout tropical monsoon climates, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu.
varieties.availability: Coconut Development Board, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, government nurseries throughout tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Tiptur Tall (TPT)
varieties.traits: Tall variety, very low maintenance, one of easiest coconuts to grow. Large leathery fronds.
varieties.yield_notes: 70-80 coconuts per year, starts bearing 6-7 years after planting
varieties.growing_notes: Famous for minimal maintenance requirements. Reliable year-after-year production. Well-suited to Tropical monsoon climate. Good choice for low-input farming.
varieties.availability: Available in tropical monsoon climates from agricultural departments, regional nurseries

varieties.name: Andaman Ordinary Tall
varieties.traits: Very tall variety up to 30 meters, robust growth, good toddy yielder
varieties.growing_notes: Farmers plant in large numbers especially in Karnataka. Well-adapted to island and coastal tropical conditions, performs well in Tropical monsoon monsoon climate.
varieties.availability: Government nurseries, Coconut Development Board

varieties.name: Chowghat Orange Dwarf (COD / Chenthangu)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates, India - known as 'Chenthangu' in Malayalam
varieties.traits: True dwarf variety 12-16 feet height, early flowering 3-4 years. Round to almost-round fruits with smooth orange skin when ripe. Compact size ideal for small holdings.

varieties.name: Chowghat Green Dwarf (CGD)
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety with dark green leaves, petioles, and nuts. Takes 3-4 years to bear.
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for tropical monsoon climates tender coconut production. Distinctive all-green appearance. Well-adapted to Tropical monsoon monsoon conditions.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, ICAR-CPCRI, Coconut Development Board nurseries

varieties.name: Chowghat Yellow Dwarf
varieties.traits: Smallest coconut tree variety, vibrant yellow-colored coconuts
varieties.growing_notes: Highly resistant to diseases and pests. Easier maintenance. Perfect for small gardens and home cultivation in tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries, agricultural departments

varieties.name: Chowghat Red Dwarf
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety 25-30 feet height, distinctive red-tinged nuts. Thrives in various soil types including sandy and loamy.
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent pest and disease resistance. Low-maintenance option. Prized for sweet water and distinctive appearance. Ideal for Tropical monsoon humid tropical climate.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, specialized nurseries

varieties.name: Malayan Yellow Dwarf (MYD)
varieties.traits: Medium dwarf 30-50 feet, pale yellow petioles and mature fruit. Early bearing 3-4 years, 50-year lifespan.
varieties.growing_notes: Widely grown globally, performs excellently in Tropical monsoon hot tropical climate. High yields in consistently hot areas. Disease resistant. Ideal for Tropical monsoon monsoon conditions with high heat and humidity.
varieties.availability: Available from Greens of tropical monsoon climates, Indian Glorious Nursery, tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Malayan Green Dwarf
varieties.traits: Shorter dwarf maximum 30 feet, stays green even when fully ripe

varieties.name: Malayan Orange Dwarf
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf variety, vibrant orange-colored coconuts
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent disease resistance ensures healthy, strong trees. Popular among home gardeners and commercial growers in tropical monsoon climates due to compact size and high yield.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries, Mahindra Nursery, Kadiyam Nursery

varieties.name: Kalpasree
varieties.traits: EARLIEST flowering cultivar - 2.5-3 years for flowering. Dwarf variety. Root wilt disease tolerant.
varieties.growing_notes: Released specifically for cultivation in root wilt prevalent areas of tropical monsoon climates. Disease resistance critical for Tropical monsoon conditions. Exceptionally fast to production. Excellent choice for tropical monsoon climates and Pathanamthitta District where root wilt can be an issue.
varieties.availability: ICAR-CPCRI stations, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, Coconut Development Board

varieties.name: Kalpa Jyothi
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety with yellow fruits
varieties.yield_notes: 114 nuts per palm per year under rainfed conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended specifically for tropical monsoon climates and Karnataka for tender nut purpose. Higher average yield than many dwarfs. Performs well even without irrigation in Tropical monsoon high-rainfall monsoon climate.
varieties.availability: ICAR-CPCRI, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University

varieties.name: Kalpa Suvarna
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety, dual-purpose (copra and tender nut). Commences flowering 30-36 months after planting. Regular bearer.
varieties.yield_notes: 97 nuts per palm per year (17,169 nuts per hectare per year)

varieties.name: Kalparaksha
varieties.traits: Semi-tall variety with higher resistance to root wilt disease. Flowering by 54 months (4.5 years).
varieties.yield_notes: 87 nuts per palm in normal conditions; 65 nuts per palm in root wilt affected tracts
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically released for tender nut production AND cultivation in root wilt prevalent areas of tropical monsoon climates. Critical for tropical monsoon climates/Pathanamthitta region where root wilt disease is a concern. Disease resistance plus good productivity makes it excellent choice for tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.availability: ICAR-CPCRI Kayamkulam station (near tropical monsoon climates), tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University

varieties.name: Kalpa Dhenu (Tall Variety)
varieties.traits: Superior high-yielding tall selection, lesser eriophyid mite infestation
varieties.yield_notes: 118 nuts per palm per year under rainfed conditions

varieties.name: Kalpa Prathibha (Tall Variety)
varieties.traits: Tall variety characterized by large fruits with higher volume tender nut water

varieties.name: Kalpa Ratna (Tall Variety)
varieties.traits: High-yielding tall variety suitable for multiple purposes: tender nut, copra, AND inflorescence sap (neera) production
varieties.yield_notes: 133 nuts per palm per year - exceptionally high

varieties.name: Kalpa Vajra (Tall Variety - WCT Selection)
varieties.origin: West Coast Tall × West Coast Tall progenies, selected from high-yielding root wilt disease-free WCT palms
varieties.traits: Superior selection of traditional WCT with enhanced disease resistance and higher yield
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yield than standard WCT with less root wilt disease incidence
varieties.growing_notes: Based on performance trials at CDB Farm Neriamangalam and ICAR-CPCRI Kayamkulam (both in tropical monsoon climates). Selected specifically for tropical monsoon climates conditions from the indigenous West Coast Tall. Maintains WCT characteristics while improving disease resistance and yield. Perfect for tropical monsoon climates region.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, ICAR-CPCRI Kayamkulam, Coconut Development Board Neriamangalam

varieties.name: Kerasree (WCT × MYD)
varieties.origin: Released by tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, 1992
varieties.traits: Dwarf hybrid (West Coast Tall × Malayan Yellow Dwarf), starts bearing 5th year
varieties.yield_notes: 130-150 fruits per tree - considered best variety among Dwarf coconuts

varieties.name: Chandra Sankara (COD × WCT)
varieties.origin: India (Chowghat Orange Dwarf × West Coast Tall)
varieties.traits: Early bearing hybrid compared to tall palms. Copra 160-230g per nut.

varieties.name: Keraganga (WCT × Gangabondam)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates (West Coast Tall × Gangabondam)
varieties.traits: Hybrid combining Tropical monsoon indigenous WCT with early-bearing Gangabondam
varieties.growing_notes: Released specifically for tropical monsoon climates. Excellent copra and oil production. Combines local adaptation of WCT with early bearing of Gangabondam.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, government nurseries

varieties.name: Kerasowbhagya (WCT × SSA)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates (West Coast Tall × SSA)
varieties.traits: Hybrid variety
varieties.growing_notes: Released for tropical monsoon climates cultivation. Uses indigenous WCT as parent.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University

varieties.name: Kera Keralam (WCT Selection)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates - 'Kera Keralam' literally means 'Coconut of tropical monsoon climates'
varieties.traits: Selection from traditional West Coast Tall
varieties.growing_notes: Released by Coconut Research Station Veppankulam for tropical monsoon climates, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal. Name celebrates Tropical monsoon coconut heritage. High-yielding selection of the traditional WCT that is the backbone of tropical monsoon climates coconut cultivation.
varieties.availability: Available online at Cocoter Farm, tropical monsoon climates nurseries, agricultural stations

varieties.name: Kalyani Coconut-1
varieties.traits: High-yielding variety

varieties.name: Anandaganga (Andaman Ordinary × Gangabondam)
varieties.traits: Hybrid combining island and mainland varieties
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Tropical monsoon tropical climate. Combines tropical island genetics with early bearing.
varieties.availability: Government nurseries

varieties.name: Godavari Ganga (ECT × Gangabondam)
varieties.traits: Hybrid variety

varieties.name: VHC1 (ECT × Malayan Green Dwarf)
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu, India (East Coast Tall × Malayan Green Dwarf)
varieties.traits: Famous hybrid for massive coconut production, early bearing 4 years, disease-resistant, intercropping-friendly

varieties.name: Kalpa Ganga (COD × Gangabondam)
varieties.traits: Hybrid combining Tropical monsoon COD with Gangabondam early bearing
varieties.yield_notes: 167 nuts per palm per year - extremely high
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended for tropical monsoon climates and Karnataka. Suitable for tender nut purpose. Exceptional yields make it excellent for commercial cultivation.
varieties.availability: ICAR-CPCRI, tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University

varieties.name: Kalpa Shreyas (COD × West Asian Tall)
varieties.origin: ICAR-CPCRI (Chowghat Orange Dwarf × West Asian Tall)
varieties.traits: DxT hybrid, high nut yield
varieties.yield_notes: 151 coconuts per palm per year, 26,497 nuts per ha
varieties.growing_notes: Triple purpose: tender nut, copra, and neera/toddy production. Uses Tropical monsoon indigenous COD as parent. Excellent for traditional tropical monsoon climates coconut industries.
varieties.availability: ICAR-CPCRI

varieties.name: King Coconut
varieties.traits: Moderate height to 10 meters, distinctive orange-yellow fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Common along roads in southern India. Prized for nutritional tender coconut water. Well-suited to Tropical monsoon tropical climate. Popular for health-conscious consumers.
varieties.availability: South Indian nurseries, tropical monsoon climates suppliers

varieties.name: Macapuno (Kopyor)
varieties.traits: Dwarf mutant tree, abnormal endosperm development
varieties.growing_notes: Prized specialty coconut for desserts and sweets. Commands premium prices. Popular in Asian desserts. Would grow well in Tropical monsoon tropical climate. Cultural significance in regions where grown.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, may need importation from Philippines

varieties.name: Jamaican Tall
varieties.traits: Very tall to 33 meters, beautiful fast-growing palm, lives 80 years. Dense globose canopy with huge dark green leaves. Swollen base trunk.
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific - up to 200 coconuts per year
varieties.growing_notes: Extremely high productivity. Distinctive appearance. Suitable for Tropical monsoon consistently hot tropical climate. Kalpasree or Kalparaksha - root wilt resistant
varieties.availability: May require specialty import
